title: PNGase F Protocol (Denaturing Conditions)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c24eu","text":"PNGase F is the most effective enzymatic method for removing almost allN-linked oligosaccharides from glycoproteins. PNGase F is an amidase, which cleaves between the innermost GlcNAc and asparagine residues of high mannose, hybrid, and complex oligosaccharides. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":71,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4vj7n","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dkldc","text":"This is a generic PNGase F protocol for denaturing reaction conditions. It is appropriate for both P0704 and P0708. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":40,"length":30}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":99,"length":5},{"key":1,"offset":109,"length":5}],"data":{}},{"key":"2drn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ipswe","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"ertp7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"D00D7146C61D11EA92280A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://www.neb.com/products/p0704-pngase-f"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"D00D71E1C61D11EA92280A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://www.neb.com/products/p0708-pngase-f-recombinant"}},"2":{"type":"image","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"62F53CC4341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","height":343,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":null,\"entityMap\":null}","mime":"image/png","original_name":"image.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/files/gmeg9nan.jpg","source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/gmee9nan.jpg","type":1,"webp_placeholder":"https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/files/gmef9nan.webp","webp_source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/gmed9nan.webp","width":550}}}}

steps: Combine 1 µg - 20 µg, glycoprotein , 1 µL, Glycoprotein Denaturing Buffer (10X) and H 2 O (if necessary) to make a 10 µL total reaction volume .
Denature glycoprotein by heating reaction at 100 °C for 00:10:00 .
Chill denatured glycoprotein On ice and centrifuge 00:00:10 .

Make a total reaction volume of 20 µL by adding: 2 µL, GlycoBuffer 2 (10X) , 2 µL, 10% NP-40 and 6 µL, H2O . PNGase F is inhibited by SDS, therefore it is essential to have NP-40 in the reaction mixture under denaturing conditions. Failure to include NP-40 into the denaturing protocol will result in loss of enzymatic activity.
Add 1 µL, PNGase F and mix gently.
Analyze by method of choice. Note: The simplest method of assessing the extent of deglycosylation is by mobility shifts on SDS-PAGE gels.
creator: New England   Biolabs
